name: the-education-of-a-value-investor
description: Guy Spier's The Education of a Value Investor — apply value investing principles to both money and life: think independently, stay within your circle of competence, avoid biases, and cultivate long-term temperament
useCase: Helps anyone become a better investor by adopting the principles of value investing — margin of safety, circle of competence, long-term thinking, and the temperament to ignore short-term noise.
knownRisks: Value investing requires patience and conviction — it's not suited for short-term traders
